questions.question: All the following are forces in the external environment of business except ______.
questions.explanation: External forces are things outside the business like politics, laws, and technology. Employees are inside the business, not outside.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Environmental Influences on Entrepreneurship

questions.question: An entrepreneur who starts a new business after abandoning a previous business is called ______.
questions.explanation: Serial means doing something again and again. A serial entrepreneur keeps starting new businesses, one after another.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of starting a small business?
options: Lack of continuity after owner dies
questions.explanation: When the owner dies and the business ends, that is a bad thing. Benefits are good things like control, low cost, and keeping profits.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Small Business

questions.question: Entrepreneurs contribute to national development in the following way except ______.
questions.explanation: Avoiding taxes is illegal and hurts the country. Entrepreneurs help by creating jobs, building capital, and making new things.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Role of Entrepreneurship in Economic Development

questions.question: The characteristics within an individual that causes him to behave in a consistent way overtime is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Your personality is who you are deep down. It stays the same over time and makes you act in certain ways.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Entrepreneurial Characteristics

questions.question: The process of setting up your own business starts with ______.
questions.explanation: You cannot start a business until you first spot a chance to do something people want. That is the opportunity.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - The Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: The factors that affect the entrepreneurship process include the following except ______.
questions.explanation: The book says three approaches are used: psychological, sociological, and situational. Cultural is not one of them.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Factors that Influence the Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: Which of the following is not a psychological factor that is believed to affect an individual's decision of becoming an entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: Negative displacement is about society pushing you out. It is not about your mind or feelings, so it is sociological.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Psychological and Sociological Influences

questions.question: Businesses with more than ten (10) employees, according to Ghana Statistical Services, are classified as ______.
questions.explanation: In Ghana, if you have more than 10 workers, you are called a small business. Micro businesses have fewer workers.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 5 - Classification of Small Businesses in Ghana

questions.question: Focus groups are ______.
options: groups who meet to discuss and generate new ideas
questions.explanation: A focus group is when people sit together to talk and come up with fresh ideas for a business.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Sources and Techniques for Business Ideas

questions.question: The following are some reasons why small businesses fail at start up except ______.
options: lack of technical and managerial skills
questions.explanation: Having a clear idea helps you succeed. The other options (bad timing, no way to sell products, no skills) cause failure.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 4 - Causes of New Venture Failure

questions.question: Which of the following is not an approach for evaluating new ventures?
options: Determining the technical feasibility of idea
Analysis of organisational resources and capabilities
questions.explanation: You check money, technology, and your team's ability. Systems analysis is not used for this.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Evaluating New Venture Opportunities

questions.question: In preparing to buy a new business, it is necessary to conduct a self-audit before the decision is launched. The purpose of the self-audit is to ______.
options: determine your strengths and weaknesses and the ideal business that matches the strengths
determine opportunities and threats in the business environment
evaluate the performance of the potential business
knock on the doors of the potential businesses
questions.explanation: Self-audit means looking at yourself first. Know what you are good at and what you are bad at before looking at businesses.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Buying an Existing Business

questions.question: Advantages of buying existing businesses include the following except ______.
questions.explanation: Poor goodwill means customers do not like the business. That is a disadvantage, not an advantage.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Buying a Business

questions.question: The business arrangement where one party grants the other the right and license to use its trade name as well as business processes is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Franchising is when you pay to use another company's name and way of doing business. Think of KFC or MTN shops.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Meaning and Nature of Franchising

questions.question: The type of franchising, where franchisees are granted the license to sell specific products under the manufacturer's brand and trade name through selected distribution channels is known as ______.
questions.explanation: This is like a car dealership. You sell a specific brand's products, not the whole business system.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Types of Franchising

questions.question: A part of a business plan that is the last to be written but it is always placed at the beginning of the plan is the ______.
questions.explanation: The executive summary is a short version of the whole plan. You write it last, but put it first so readers see it right away.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 2 - Structure of the Business Plan

questions.question: People who are in 'between things' are not likely to seek entrepreneurial opportunities.
questions.explanation: This is false. People between jobs or schools often start businesses because they are looking for a new path.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Sociological Influences on Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Copyright franchising is one of the main types of franchising agreement.
questions.explanation: This is false. The main types are product distribution, trade name, and pure franchising. Copyright is not one of them.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Franchising Agreements

questions.question: Business plan is not necessary when purchasing an already established company.
questions.explanation: This is false. Even if a business already exists, you still need a plan to check its finances, risks, and future.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Importance of Business Planning
